project.name: ar-tata-surya
project.scope: Bugfix: camera layering, marker-relative GLB scale normalization, GLB material texture recovery, compact focus overlays, safe AR-to-landing cleanup, and debug telemetry. No visual redesign.
assumptionsToValidate: AR.js injects and sizes a camera video element into the DOM.
Camera permissions are granted before AR start.
GLB assets may have inconsistent source dimensions and pivot centers.
Some planet GLBs may use legacy material extensions that need runtime texture recovery.

motionPaletteDecision.densityDecisionSource: Compact focus controls, container-relative AR layer fitting, and debug-only scale/material telemetry keep the camera view dominant.
motionPaletteDecision.paletteAutopilotRisk: avoid opaque overlays or forced renderer dimensions that hide camera feed
motionPaletteDecision.spatialDecision: 3d-canvas-required-for-ar-scene; model scale is normalized with bounding boxes before display and material identity is recovered when GLB maps are missing

conceptualAnchor.name: Optical Viewfinder Overlay
conceptualAnchor.anchorReference: Optical viewfinder with matte box and HUD markings
conceptualAnchor.specificReferencePoint: camera viewfinder frame and matte box
conceptualAnchor.rationale: Layering, occlusion control, model fit, material identity, and page cleanup should mimic a viewfinder: camera feed is base, overlays are transparent and precise, and the object stays readable inside the marker frame.
conceptualAnchor.signatureMotion: status overlays fade in/out quickly while GLB objects rotate slowly from an identity-first orientation inside a bounded marker-relative frame
conceptualAnchor.typographicDecision: display for titles, body for instructions, monospace for debug data

derivedTokenLogic.anchorReference: Optical viewfinder with matte box and HUD markings
derivedTokenLogic.morphology: thin overlay frames and transparent layers
derivedTokenLogic.motion: short fades for status and slow object rotation for spatial comprehension

derivedTokenLogic.anchorReference: Optical viewfinder with matte box and HUD markings
derivedTokenLogic.colorDerivationSource: Existing dark UI overlays are retained, but camera feed stays visible and is not covered by renderer fallback black.
derivedTokenLogic.spacingDerivationSource: Spacing favors top chip scanning and bottom compact disclosure to keep the marker center unobstructed.
derivedTokenLogic.typographyDerivationSource: Display and body roles are preserved for clarity in small screens.
derivedTokenLogic.motionDerivationSource: Short status fades and slow model rotation follow the optical viewfinder anchor without decorative overlay motion.
derivedTokenLogic.validationRule: No overlay, viewport calculation, normalized GLB, or AR cleanup path should hide the camera feed, crop the AR page width, leave stale AR state on landing, or erase planet texture identity when video is ready.

libraryDecisions.name: A-Frame
libraryDecisions.fallbackIfUnavailable: block AR with a clear error message

libraryDecisions.name: AR.js
libraryDecisions.fallbackIfUnavailable: block AR with a clear error message

aiSafeUiAudit.rationale: This change preserves the existing UI and focuses on camera visibility and layering.

repoEvidence.designEvidenceSummary: Existing AR UI uses glass overlays and loading/error panels; no redesign requested.

notes: Overlays remain readable on top of live video.
No new motion beyond status transitions.
